name: Proto-Tamil Hypothesis (VPS 2024)
publication: Ancient Harappans Speak! After 5000 years (2024); Indus Script is a Language (2026)
description: Maps Indus graphemes to Tamil phonemes/morphemes. Tally marks as phonetic placeholders. Urban topology hypothesis for motifs.

name: Proto-Dravidian Hypothesis (Parpola)
publication: Deciphering the Indus Script. Cambridge University Press, 1994.
description: Rebus principle mapping to Proto-Dravidian. Most widely accepted academic framework.

name: Dravidian Hypothesis (Mahadevan)
publication: The Indus Script: Texts, Concordance and Tables. ASI, 1977.
description: Foundational corpus concordance. Sign numbering standard still in use.

name: Non-Linguistic Emblem Thesis (Farmer-Sproat-Witzel)
researcher: Steve Farmer, Richard Sproat, Michael Witzel
publication: The Collapse of the Indus Script Thesis. EJVS, 2004.
description: Signs are political/religious emblems, not phonetic. High singleton rate as evidence. Primary null hypothesis.

name: Dravidian Statistical (Rao et al.)
publication: Entropic Evidence for Linguistic Structure in the Indus Script. Science, 2009.
description: Entropy/n-gram analysis shows Indus signs have linguistic structure. Does not claim specific language.

name: Proto-Sanskrit Hypothesis (Rajaram-Jha)
publication: The Deciphered Indus Script. Aditya Prakashan, 1996.
description: Claims script encodes Sanskrit. Largely discredited after horse-seal fabrication controversy (2000).
